title: IT Operating Model (ITOM)
lead: The IT Operating Model describes how an organisation's IT function is structured and managed to deliver business value — the 'how' that translates IT strategy into daily operations.

sections.heading: The IT Operating Model describes how IT is organised to effectively deliver on strategy.
sections.alt: ITOM pyramid — Strategy, IT Operating Model, Policy, Process, Technology and People

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: The IT Operating Model (ITOM) sits between IT strategy and daily operations. IT strategy defines 'what you are going to do' — the goals, priorities, and direction. The IT Operating Model defines 'how you are going to do it' — the processes, governance, organisation, sourcing, and technology that make delivery possible. Without a well-designed ITOM, even the best strategy fails in execution. The ITOM is the design blueprint that makes strategy operational.

sections.heading: The Atos ITOM Framework
sections.alt: The Atos ITOM Framework — five levels from Vision & Strategy to Delivery Support

items.desc: business strategy, digital strategy, IT strategy, and cloud direction shape the operating model scope and ambition
enterprise architecture, functional design, and outsourcing approach define the high-level structure
the detailed design of functions, processes, governance models, and organisational modes
performance management, stakeholder relationships, and organisational structure brought to life
technology, methodology, finance, security, and supply chain that underpin daily operations

sections.heading: Alternative ITOM Topologies
sections.alt: Five ITOM topologies from Complete Autonomy to Platformized Digital Process along the digital maturity axis

items.desc: IT projects and infrastructure managed independently per business domain — maximum autonomy, minimum standardisation
shared infrastructure and strategic IT governance with domain-level autonomy for applications and projects
structured domains with shared services, common governance, and coordinated delivery across the organisation
business and IT domains aligned around shared platforms, common infrastructure, and joint governance
fully integrated platform model — shared IT foundations, automated operations, and product-centric delivery at scale

sections.heading: The Atos Approach to ITOM
sections.alt: Atos ITOM delivery approach — flexible waterfall or agile, tailored to situation

items.desc: shape the Target Operating Model strategy and programme, informed by business drivers, digital ambition, and IT strategy
develop and assess operating model capabilities and options, guided by business context and constraints
create the overall design for role and size of the future IT organisation — the Target Operating Model
structure and execute the implementation plan to deliver the new operating model
define and quantify the business benefits and value the new operating model enables
ITOM assessment — evaluate the current IT operating model across all five framework levels
Target Operating Model design — define the future-state ITOM aligned to business strategy
ITOM Canvas workshops — co-create the operating model with business and IT stakeholders
Topology selection — determine the right ITOM topology based on organisational context and digital maturity
Transformation roadmap — plan the journey from current to target operating model
